{"step_by_step_thinking": "The documents clearly indicate that ultrasound (USG) is the preferred initial imaging modality for children due to its safety profile, absence of ionizing radiation, cost-effectiveness, and high sensitivity and specificity. MRI is also mentioned as a reliable diagnostic tool, but it is less commonly used due to its higher cost, longer scan times, and the need for skilled interpretation. CT scans, while highly accurate, involve ionizing radiation and are thus less preferred in children. X-ray is not mentioned as a diagnostic tool for appendicitis.", "answer_choice": "C"}
